name: Accessibility Remap Controls
description: A control settings interface that lets players rebind input actions across keyboard, mouse, gamepad, touch, or accessibility devices with conflict handling and persistence.
design_notes: Control remapping is an input profile workflow. Define input_action_id, input_binding_id, device profile, conflict policy, reserved system actions, reset defaults, per-player local co-op ownership, and persistence. The UI should preview the binding, explain conflicts, and keep gameplay prompts synchronized with the active mapping.
design_purpose: Let players adapt controls to their device, body, handedness, and preference.
Keep gameplay prompts, tutorials, and action state aligned with the current input profile.
Prevent inaccessible or broken layouts through conflict rules, reserved bindings, and reset flows.
player_fantasy: The player feels the interface respects their preferred device and lets them make controls usable without breaking the game.

parameters.name: input_binding_id
parameters.description: Device-specific binding assigned to an action.

parameters.name: remap_conflict_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule applied when a new binding already belongs to another action.

parameters.name: control_scheme_id
parameters.description: Device profile that owns the binding set.

parameters.name: reserved_action_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule for platform, OS, or safety-critical reserved inputs.

parameters.name: binding_profile_save_version
parameters.description: Version used to migrate saved bindings after input changes.

edge_cases: A player unbinds confirm, cancel, pause, or menu navigation and needs a recovery path.
Two local players remap controls on one device and must not overwrite each other.
A platform reserves a button or key that the game exposes in its default binding list.
Localization changes action labels while saved input_action_id values must remain stable.
common_bugs: Prompt glyphs keep showing default bindings after the player remaps input.
Conflict resolution updates the visible row but not the actual input map consumed by gameplay.
Reset defaults applies to keyboard but leaves gamepad or touch bindings stale.
Saved bindings from an old version map to the wrong action after input_action_id renames.
balancing_notes: Remapping should generally be allowed in ranked modes because it changes access, not action strength.
Some competitive games may restrict macros or multi-action bindings, but should explain that boundary clearly.
Preset layouts are useful, but full remapping is needed for many accessibility devices and preferences.
accessibility_notes: Support keyboard, mouse, gamepad, touch, and adaptive controller profiles where the platform allows it.
Keep remap UI navigable without relying on the binding being changed; always provide reset and cancel paths.

implementation_notes.unity: Use the Input System action map as the source of input_action_id records, then store overrides per accessibility_profile_id or local player. UI Toolkit rows should validate conflicts, write profile data, and refresh prompt glyphs immediately.
implementation_notes.godot: Resolve remaps through InputMap action names but persist stable input_action_id values and device profiles separately. Use a remap capture mode with timeout, cancel, conflict preview, and default reset per scheme.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Enhanced Input mapping contexts with per-player saved key profiles. UMG should apply remap_conflict_policy_id before committing, then rebuild input glyphs and local player mappings.
implementation_notes.web: Normalize KeyboardEvent, PointerEvent, Gamepad API, and touch bindings into input_binding_id records. Persist per-profile mappings and apply them before simulation code reads action state.

relationships.reason: Remap screens should expose hold-or-toggle policies alongside binding changes for sustained actions.
Prompts need to display the current binding glyph or action label after remapping.
Rhythm games need calibration and binding changes to avoid shifting judgment windows unexpectedly.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Includes remappable controls across controller and keyboard inputs.

example_games.title: The Last of Us Part II
example_games.note: Includes extensive control and accessibility remapping options.
